Import TensorFlow 2.11 and necessary modules such as os, subprocess, and multiprocessing to manage multi-worker execution
Set up environment variables for PYTHONPATH to include the TensorFlow models directory and DATA_DIR pointing to a local or accessible ImageNet TFRecord dataset

Prepare two separate YAML config files (gpu.yaml and gpu1.yaml) with the following contents: runtime.distribution_strategy='multi_worker_mirrored', runtime.worker_hosts='ip1:6655,ip2:6655', runtime.num_gpus=2, and task_index=0 for gpu.yaml and 1 for gpu1.yaml respectively
On two separate nodes or machines (with IP addresses ip1 and ip2), allocate 2 GPUs each and confirm CUDA 11.0 with compatible NVIDIA drivers and TensorFlow-GPU 2.11 are installed
On node ip1, activate the conda environment containing TensorFlow 2.11 and set the MODEL_DIR to a writeable checkpoint directory; similarly on node ip2 with the same environment and MODEL_DIR path

Launch the training script classifier_trainer.py on both nodes simultaneously using the respective config files: on ip1 run with gpu.yaml (task_index=0), on ip2 run with gpu1.yaml (task_index=1), passing identical command line arguments including --mode=train_and_eval, --model_type=resnet, --dataset=imagenet, --model_dir, --data_dir, and --config_file

Ensure the ResNet50 model is used by confirming the --model_type=resnet argument and that the training uses batch sizes and image sizes consistent with ImageNet standards (e.g., batch size per GPU ~64, image size 224x224)
Monitor the logs output by both nodes to verify that the MultiWorkerMirroredStrategy initializes correctly and that both workers join the cluster using the given worker_hosts
Record the throughput (examples/sec) reported in the logs during training on each node and aggregate to compute total throughput
Validate that the total throughput is approximately equal to single-GPU throughput (around 270 examples/sec), indicating no scaling across nodes despite using 4 GPUs total (2 GPUs per node * 2 nodes)

Confirm that running the same training on a single node with 2 GPUs using MirroredStrategy (not MultiWorkerMirroredStrategy) shows expected scaling (around 700 examples/sec), to rule out intra-node scaling issues
Optionally, run nvidia-smi or similar GPU monitoring tools on both nodes during training to confirm that all GPUs are active and utilized
Collect and save all logs and throughput metrics for comparison and reproducibility
Assert that the multi-node multi-GPU training throughput does not increase compared to single-GPU baseline, thus reproducing the reported scaling bug
